memoryArtifacts.headline: John Warner b abt 1530
memoryArtifacts.description: John Warner is the father of John Warner and the grandfather of Andrew Warner. He resided in Great Waltham, or, as it is called in some wills, "Much Waltham," Essex County, England. In his will, dated …

memoryArtifacts.headline: John Warner I Will part 1
memoryArtifacts.description: Will of John Warner I page 1

popularStory.headline: John Warner b abt 1530 om Essex. England
popularStory.description:
John Warner Sr. was born about 1530 in Great Waltham, Essex, England. He married Margaret. He died May 1584 in Haddern, Heretshire, England. He was buried in the churchyard of Much Waltham.

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Warner於1546年4月25日在英國艾可格亞特 沃爾坦受洗時，他的父親Capt. Thomas S. Warner32歲，母親Joan Baker44歲。他於1555年與Margaret在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1584年5月23日在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 沃爾坦享年38歲。

nameMeanings.name: Warner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Warnier (Old French Garnier). Compare Garner and Werner .
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Warrener (see Warren 2).

Irish (Cork): when this is not the Anglo-Norman name (see above), an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane ), found in medieval records as Iwarrynane, from a genitive or plural form of the name, in which m is lenited.

variants.name: Garner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps occasionally from the Old French personal name Garnier (see Garnier ), but it is exeptionally rare as a personal name in medieval England and no certain evidence has been found for its use as a surname. Compare Warner .

English: from Middle English gern(i)er, garner, gurner, Anglo-Norman French gerner ‘granary’ (Old French grenier, from Late Latin granarium, a derivative of granum ‘grain’). It was probably a metonymic occupational name for someone in charge of the stores kept in a granary.
English and Scottish: commonly shortened form of   Gardner  .

variants.name: Werner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Gerhard, Ernst, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Dieter.

German, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Hungarian, Norwegian, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The surname of German origin is also found in Britain (compare Warner ) and France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Verner .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Hungarian, Swedish, Danish, Croatian, and Slovenian   Verner  , a cognate or variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When John Warner was christened on 25 April 1546, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, his father, Capt. Thomas S. Warner, was 32 and his mother, Joan Baker, was 44. He married Margaret in 1555, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. He died on 23 May 1584, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38.

Warner, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Joan Baker, aveva 44 anni. Lui sposato Margaret nel 1555, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 23 maggio 1584, nel Great Waltham, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 38 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Warnerが1546年4月25日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムで洗礼を受けた時，父Capt. Thomas S. Warnerは32歳，母Joan Bakerは44歳であった。1555年にイギリスでMargaretと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1584年5月23日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムで38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Warner(이)가 1546년 4월 25일 영국 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Capt. Thomas S. Warner(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Baker(은)는 44세였다. 그는 1555년 영국에서 Margaret(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1584년 5월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Warner foi batizado em 25 de abril de 1546, em Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Capt. Thomas S. Warner, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Joan Baker, tinha 44 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret em 1555, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de maio de 1584, em Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 38 anos.

ru.Hatfield Broad Oak, Essex, England, Unit: хатфиелд броад оак, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Warner был крещен 25 апреля 1546 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Capt. Thomas S. Warner было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Baker, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret в 1555 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей. Он умер 23 мая 1584 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Warner se bautizó el 25 de abril de 1546, en Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Capt. Thomas S. Warner, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Joan Baker, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Margaret en 1555, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 23 de mayo de 1584, en Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 38 años.
